text: Radical cross-coupling has become a powerful method for forming C(sp3)–C and C(sp3)–heteroatom bonds. Most of the related transition metal catalytic reactions are accompanied by photocatalysis or electrocatalysis. Hence, there is limited exploration of main group elements towards this subject. In this work, the low-valent complex of bismuth acts as a site to catalyze the one-electron oxidative addition with the redox-active alkyl radical precursors thus providing a unique opportunity to mimic the transition metals from first row. Consequently, the Bi(III)–C(sp3) oxidative addition complexes could be provided in solution and in solid state. A differentiation of reactivity towards the alkyl radical precursors can be observed based on the electronic effects induced by α-substituents. These mechanistic studies not only provided the clues for the bismuth-catalyzed cross-coupling between two disparate moieties but also led to the design and exploration of a bismuth-catalyzed C(sp3)–N cross-coupling reactions. The as-designed reactions were performed under very mild conditions and gave high yields of diverse important heterocyclic amine products.
